mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held at or near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins accelerating above its 12-month trend, real rates turn negative. Negative real rates compress equity risk premiums and boost forward equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with accommodative policy and rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger is active, or positive forward returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 200k signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in < 58% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1.4 million annualized units signals weakening demand for residential construction and durable goods, which reduces forward GDP growth expectations and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1.4 million units for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in < 50% of instances where trigger is active, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling a supply-side shock or demand destruction that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month, indicating strong consumer demand and forward earnings momentum. This drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations expand on improved growth expectations.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 6.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) contracts month-over-month, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 1.0% while real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized, indicating monetary policy is accommodative relative to economic growth. This supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with above-trend growth
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 62% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.045 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines for 2 consecutive months while inflation (CPIAUCSL) continues rising, signaling stagflation conditions where supply constraints persist despite weakening demand. This compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0% for 2 consecutive months AND CPIAUCSL 3-month change > 0.8%
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in < 54% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
